instruction:
Set up the PostgreSQL database layer for Ship. Create a `db/` directory under the API package with: `client.ts` (pg Pool with connection pooling, idle timeout, statement timeout, graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT), `schema.sql` (the full schema), and a `migrations/` folder for incremental changes.

Important path constraint: the canonical deliverable must live under `api/db/`, not only under the legacy `api/src/db/` path that may already exist in the repo. If existing runtime imports still point at `api/src/db/*`, convert those files into thin wrappers or re-exports only after the real implementation exists under `api/db/*`. When converting a legacy file into a wrapper, replace the entire file contents so only the wrapper/re-export remains; do not prepend exports onto the old implementation body. Do not treat editing only `api/src/db/*` as completion.

The schema must implement Ship's "everything is a document" model. Core tables: `workspaces`, `users` (with email, password_hash, x509_subject_dn, is_super_admin), `workspace_memberships` (role: admin|member), `sessions` (TEXT primary key from crypto.randomBytes, with last_activity and ip_address), `documents` (unified table with document_type ENUM for wiki/issue/program/project/sprint/person/weekly_plan/weekly_retro/standup/weekly_review, JSONB properties, JSONB content for TipTap, BYTEA yjs_state, parent_id self-reference with circular-reference prevention trigger, visibility private|workspace, soft-delete via deleted_at, ticket_number auto-increment per workspace), `document_associations` (junction table with relationship_type ENUM: parent/project/sprint/program), `document_history`, `document_snapshots`, `audit_logs`, `comments` (threaded with parent_id), `files`, `document_links` (for backlinks), `api_tokens`, `workspace_invites`, `oauth_state`, `sprint_iterations`, and `issue_iterations`. Add pg_trgm extension and comprehensive indexes (GIN on properties, trigram on titles, partial indexes on archived_at/deleted_at).

Write a `seed.ts` that creates a demo workspace, admin user (password hashed with bcryptjs), and sample documents. Write a `migrate.ts` that applies SQL files from `migrations/` in order. The initial canonical migration file must be `api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql`. Verify by running `pnpm db:migrate` and `pnpm db:seed` against a local PostgreSQL instance, then querying each table to confirm it exists with correct columns. Before declaring completion, confirm that `api/db/client.ts`, `api/db/migrate.ts`, `api/db/seed.ts`, `api/db/schema.sql`, and `api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql` all exist.

steps.description: Rework the canonical api/db TypeScript entrypoints so they do not introduce new type errors: fix broken .js import specifiers, align pg typing/usages, and simplify/repair bootstrap + migration runner wiring while preserving api/db as the real implementation location.

Update canonical ancillary db scripts/helpers that still contain bad imports or stale legacy path references so the new api/db layer is self-consistent and does not keep pointing users or runtime tooling back at api/src/db.

Keep the legacy api/src/db bridge files as thin wrappers only, but refresh them after the canonical api/db fixes so they re-export cleanly with correct .js paths and do not retain divergent implementations.

Fix direct consumers/tooling that still reference the legacy db implementation path in a non-wrapper way, so helper scripts use the canonical api/db implementation without changing the broad set of normal runtime imports that already go through api/src/db wrappers.

Reconcile the canonical schema artifacts themselves if needed so the full schema and initial migration remain present, ordered correctly under api/db, and consistent with the repaired migration/bootstrap flow.

{"success":true,"content":"     1	-- Ship Database Schema
     2	-- Everything is a Document - Unified Model
     3	-- Multi-Workspace Architecture
     4	
     5	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
     6	
     7	-- Workspaces
     8	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
     9	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    10	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    11	  sprint_start_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    12	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    13	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    14	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    15	);
    16	
    17	-- Users and auth (global identity - users can belong to multiple workspaces)
    18	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    19	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    20	  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    21	  password_hash TEXT,  -- NULL if using PIV-only auth
    22	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    23	  is_super_admin BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    24	  last_workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    25	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,           -- PIV certificate X.509 Subject DN
    26	  piv_first_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ, -- When user first logged in via PIV
    27	  last_auth_provider VARCHAR(50), -- 'fpki_validator', 'caia', null (legacy)
    28	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    29	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    30	);
    31	
    32	-- Case-insensitive email uniqueness (prevents duplicate users with different casing)
    33	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS users_email_lower_unique ON users (LOWER(email));
    34	
    35	-- Workspace memberships (users can be in multiple workspaces with different roles)
    36	-- AUTHORIZATION ONLY: This table controls access. Person documents (content layer) are separate.
    37	-- Person docs link to users via properties.user_id, NOT via this table.
    38	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_memberships (
    39	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    40	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    41	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    42	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    43	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    44	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    45	  UNIQUE(workspace_id, user_id)
    46	);
    47	
    48	-- Workspace invites (email invite flow)
    49	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_invites (
    50	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    51	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    52	  email TEXT NOT NULL,
    53	  token TEXT UNIQUE,  -- NULL for PIV invites (certificate proves identity)
    54	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    55	  invited_by_user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    56	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,  -- X.509 Subject DN for PIV invites
    57	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    58	  used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    59	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    60	);
    61	
    62	-- Audit logs (compliance-grade logging)
    63	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (
    64	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    65	  workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for global actions
    66	  actor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for failed login attempts
    67	  impersonating_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- If super-admin is impersonating
    68	  action TEXT NOT NULL,
    69	  resource_type TEXT,
    70	  resource_id UUID,
    71	  details JSONB,
    72	  ip_address INET,
    73	  user_agent TEXT,
    74	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    75	);
    76	
    77	-- Sessions with 15-minute inactivity timeout and 12-hour absolute timeout
    78	-- Session ID is TEXT (hex string from crypto.randomBytes) not UUID for enhanced security
    79	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
    80	  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    81	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    82	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    83	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    84	  last_activity TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    85	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    86	  -- Session binding data for audit and security
    87	  user_agent TEXT,
    88	  ip_address TEXT
    89	);
    90	
    91	-- OAuth state (survives server restarts during auth flow)
    92	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS oauth_state (
    93	  state_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    94	  nonce TEXT NOT NULL,
    95	  code_verifier TEXT NOT NULL,
    96	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    97	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL
    98	);
    99	
   100	-- Document types enum
   101	DO $$ BEGIN
   102	  CREATE TYPE document_type AS ENUM ('wiki', 'issue', 'program', 'project', 'sprint', 'person', 'weekly_plan', 'weekly_retro', 'standup', 'weekly_review');
   103	EXCEPTION
   104	  WHEN duplicate_object THEN null;
   105	END $$;
   106	
   107	-- Core document table (unified model - EVERYTHING IS A DOCUMENT)
   108	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS documents (
   109	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   110	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   111	  document_type document_type NOT NULL DEFAULT 'wiki',
   112	  title TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Untitled',
   113	
   114	  -- TipTap JSON content stored as JSONB (shared by ALL document types)
   115	  content JSONB DEFAULT '{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph"}]}',
   116	
   117	  -- Yjs binary state for collaboration (shared by ALL document types)
   118	  yjs_state BYTEA,
   119	
   120	  -- Hierarchy (cascade delete: deleting parent deletes all children)
   121	  parent_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   122	  position INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   123	
   124	  -- Associations: program, project, and sprint relationships are stored in document_associations table
   125	  -- Legacy columns (project_id, sprint_id, program_id) were removed by migrations 027 and 029.
   126	  -- Use document_associations table for all relationship queries.
   127	
   128	  -- Type-specific properties stored as JSONB
   129	  -- Issue properties: state, priority, assignee_id, source, rejection_reason
   130	  -- Program/Project properties: color
   131	  -- Sprint properties: start_date, end_date, sprint_status, plan
   132	  -- Person properties: user_id (links to users.id), email, capacity_hours, skills
   133	  properties JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
   134	
   135	  -- Keep these as columns for indexing/relationships/sequences
   136	  ticket_number INTEGER,  -- Auto-increment per workspace, needed for display_id
   137	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- For filtering archived items
   138	  deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- For trash/soft delete (30 day retention)
   139	
   140	  -- Status timestamps (for issues)
   141	  started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,    -- When issue status first changed to in_progress
   142	  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status first changed to done
   143	  cancelled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- When issue status changed to cancelled
   144	  reopened_at TIMESTAMPTZ,   -- When issue was reopened after being done/cancelled
   145	
   146	  -- Document conversion tracking
   147	  converted_to_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,   -- Points to new doc (on archived original)
   148	  converted_from_id UUID REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE SET NULL, -- Points to original doc (on new doc)
   149	  converted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   150	  converted_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   151	  original_type VARCHAR(50),    -- Original document_type when first created
   152	  conversion_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of times converted
   153	
   154	  -- Timestamps
   155	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   156	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
   157	  created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   158	
   159	  -- Document visibility (private = creator only, workspace = all members)
   160	  visibility TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'workspace' CHECK (visibility IN ('private', 'workspace')),
   161	
   162	  -- Prevent self-referencing parent
   163	  CONSTRAINT documents_no_self_parent CHECK (id != parent_id)
   164	);
   165	
   166	-- Function and trigger to prevent circular parent references
   167	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_circular_parent()
   168	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   169	DECLARE
   170	  current_parent UUID;
   171	  depth INT := 0;
   172	  max_depth INT := 100;
   173	BEGIN
   174	  IF NEW.parent_id IS NULL THEN
   175	    RETURN NEW;
   176	  END IF;
   177	  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.parent_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NEW.parent_id THEN
   178	    RETURN NEW;
   179	  END IF;
   180	  current_parent := NEW.parent_id;
   181	  WHILE current_parent IS NOT NULL AND depth < max_depth LOOP
   182	    IF current_parent = NEW.id THEN
   183	      RAISE EXCEPTION 'Circular reference detected: document % cannot be a descendant of itself', NEW.id;
   184	    END IF;
   185	    SELECT parent_id INTO current_parent FROM documents WHERE id = current_parent;
   186	    depth := depth + 1;
   187	  END LOOP;
   188	  IF depth >= max_depth THEN
   189	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Maximum nesting depth (%) exceeded while checking for circular reference', max_depth;
   190	  END IF;
   191	  RETURN NEW;
   192	END;
   193	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

    18	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    19	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    20	  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    21	  password_hash TEXT,  -- NULL if using PIV-only auth

    33	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS users_email_lower_unique ON users (LOWER(email));

    38	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_memberships (

    49	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_invites (

    61

    63	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (

{"success":true,"content":"     1	-- Ship Database Schema
     2	-- Everything is a Document - Unified Model
     3	-- Multi-Workspace Architecture
     4	
     5	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
     6	
     7	-- Workspaces
     8	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
     9	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    10	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    11	  sprint_start_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    12	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    13	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    14	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    15	);
    16	
    17	-- Users and auth (global identity - users can belong to multiple workspaces)
    18	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    19	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    20	  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    21	  password_hash TEXT,  -- NULL if using PIV-only auth
    22	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    23	  is_super_admin BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    24	  last_workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    25	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,           -- PIV certificate X.509 Subject DN
    26	  piv_first_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ, -- When user first logged in via PIV
    27	  last_auth_provider VARCHAR(50), -- 'fpki_validator', 'caia', null (legacy)
    28	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    29	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    30	);
    31	
    32	-- Case-insensitive email uniqueness (prevents duplicate users with different casing)
    33	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS users_email_lower_unique ON users (LOWER(email));
    34	
    35	-- Workspace memberships (users can be in multiple workspaces with different roles)
    36	-- AUTHORIZATION ONLY: This table controls access. Person documents (content layer) are separate.
    37	-- Person docs link to users via properties.user_id, NOT via this table.
    38	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_memberships (
    39	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    40	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    41	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    42	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    43	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    44	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    45	  UNIQUE(workspace_id, user_id)
    46	);
    47	
    48	-- Workspace invites (email invite flow)
    49	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_invites (
    50	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    51	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    52	  email TEXT NOT NULL,
    53	  token TEXT UNIQUE,  -- NULL for PIV invites (certificate proves identity)
    54	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    55	  invited_by_user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    56	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,  -- X.509 Subject DN for PIV invites
    57	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    58	  used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    59	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    60	);
    61	
    62	-- Audit logs (compliance-grade logging)
    63	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (
    64	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    65	  workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for global actions
    66	  actor_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- NULL for failed login attempts
    67	  impersonating_user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,  -- If super-admin is impersonating
    68	  action TEXT NOT NULL,
    69	  resource_type TEXT,
    70	  resource_id UUID,
    71	  details JSONB,
    72	  ip_address INET,
    73	  user_agent TEXT,
    74	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    75	);
    76	
    77	-- Sessions with 15-minute inactivity timeout and 12-hour absolute timeout
    78	-- Session ID is TEXT (hex string from crypto.randomBytes) not UUID for enhanced security
    79	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
    80	  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    81	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    82	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    83	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    84	  last_activity TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    85	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    86	  -- Session binding data for audit and security
    87	  user_agent TEXT,
    88	  ip_address TEXT
    89	);
    90	
    91	-- OAuth state (survives server restarts during auth flow)
    92	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS oauth_state (
    93	  state_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    94	  nonce TEXT NOT NULL,
    95	  code_verifier TEXT NOT NULL,
    96	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    97	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL
    98	);
    99	
   100	-- Document types enum
   101	DO $$ BEGIN
   102	  CREATE TYPE document_type AS ENUM ('wiki', 'issue', 'program', 'project', 'sprint', 'person', 'weekly_plan', 'weekly_retro', 'standup', 'weekly_review');
   103	EXCEPTION
   104	  WHEN duplicate_object THEN null;
   105	END $$;
   106	
   107	-- Core document table (unified model - EVERYTHING IS A DOCUMENT)
   108	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS documents (
   109	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   110	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   111	  document_type document_type NOT NULL DEFAULT 'wiki',
   112	  title TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Untitled',
   113	
   114	  -- TipTap JSON content stored as JSONB (shared by ALL document types)
   115	  content JSONB DEFAULT '{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph"}]}',
   116	
   117	  -- Yjs binary state for collaboration (shared by ALL document types)
   118	  yjs_state BYTEA,
   119	
   120	  -- Hierarchy (cascade delete: deleting parent deletes all children)","total_lines":589}

{"success":true,"content":"     1	-- Ship Database Schema
     2	-- Everything is a Document - Unified Model
     3	-- Multi-Workspace Architecture
     4	
     5	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
     6	
     7	-- Workspaces
     8	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
     9	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    10	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    11	  sprint_start_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    12	  archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    13	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    14	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    15	);
    16	
    17	-- Users and auth (global identity - users can belong to multiple workspaces)
    18	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    19	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    20	  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    21	  password_hash TEXT,  -- NULL if using PIV-only auth
    22	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    23	  is_super_admin BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    24	  last_workspace_id UUID REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    25	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,           -- PIV certificate X.509 Subject DN
    26	  piv_first_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ, -- When user first logged in via PIV
    27	  last_auth_provider VARCHAR(50), -- 'fpki_validator', 'caia', null (legacy)
    28	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    29	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    30	);
    31	
    32	-- Case-insensitive email uniqueness (prevents duplicate users with different casing)
    33	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS users_email_lower_unique ON users (LOWER(email));
    34	
    35	-- Workspace memberships (users can be in multiple workspaces with different roles)
    36	-- AUTHORIZATION ONLY: This table controls access. Person documents (content layer) are separate.
    37	-- Person docs link to users via properties.user_id, NOT via this table.
    38	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_memberships (
    39	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    40	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    41	  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    42	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    43	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    44	  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    45	  UNIQUE(workspace_id, user_id)
    46	);
    47	
    48	-- Workspace invites (email invite flow)
    49	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_invites (
    50	  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    51	  workspace_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    52	  email TEXT NOT NULL,
    53	  token TEXT UNIQUE,  -- NULL for PIV invites (certificate proves identity)
    54	  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member')),
    55	  invited_by_user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    56	  x509_subject_dn TEXT,  -- X.509 Subject DN for PIV invites
    57	  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    58	  used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    59	  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    60	);
    61	
    62	-- Audit logs (compliance-grade logging)
    63	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_logs (

   166	-- Function and trigger to prevent circular parent references
   167	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_circular_parent()
   168	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   169	DECLARE
   170	  current_parent UUID;
   171	  depth INT := 0;
   172	  max_depth INT := 100;
   173	BEGIN
   174	  IF NEW.parent_id IS NULL THEN
   175	    RETURN NEW;
   176	  END IF;
   177	  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.parent_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NEW.parent_id THEN
   178	    RETURN NEW;
   179	  END IF;
   180	  current_parent := NEW.parent_id;
   181	  WHILE current_parent IS NOT NULL AND depth < max_depth LOOP
   182	    IF current_parent = NEW.id THEN
   183	      RAISE EXCEPTION 'Circular reference detected: document % cannot be a descendant of itself', NEW.id;
   184	    END IF;
   185	    SELECT parent_id INTO current_parent FROM documents WHERE id = current_parent;
   186	    depth := depth + 1;
   187	  END LOOP;
   188	  IF depth >= max_depth THEN
   189	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Maximum nesting depth (%) exceeded while checking for circular reference', max_depth;
   190	  END IF;
   191	  RETURN NEW;
   192	END;
   193	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

messages.content:
Set up the PostgreSQL database layer for Ship. Create a `db/` directory under the API package with: `client.ts` (pg Pool with connection pooling, idle timeout, statement timeout, graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT), `schema.sql` (the full schema), and a `migrations/` folder for incremental changes.

Important path constraint: the canonical deliverable must live under `api/db/`, not only under the legacy `api/src/db/` path that may already exist in the repo. If existing runtime imports still point at `api/src/db/*`, convert those files into thin wrappers or re-exports only after the real implementation exists under `api/db/*`. When converting a legacy file into a wrapper, replace the entire file contents so only the wrapper/re-export remains; do not prepend exports onto the old implementation body. Do not treat editing only `api/src/db/*` as completion.

The schema must implement Ship's "everything is a document" model. Core tables: `workspaces`, `users` (with email, password_hash, x509_subject_dn, is_super_admin), `workspace_memberships` (role: admin|member), `sessions` (TEXT primary key from crypto.randomBytes, with last_activity and ip_address), `documents` (unified table with document_type ENUM for wiki/issue/program/project/sprint/person/weekly_plan/weekly_retro/standup/weekly_review, JSONB properties, JSONB content for TipTap, BYTEA yjs_state, parent_id self-reference with circular-reference prevention trigger, visibility private|workspace, soft-delete via deleted_at, ticket_number auto-increment per workspace), `document_associations` (junction table with relationship_type ENUM: parent/project/sprint/program), `document_history`, `document_snapshots`, `audit_logs`, `comments` (threaded with parent_id), `files`, `document_links` (for backlinks), `api_tokens`, `workspace_invites`, `oauth_state`, `sprint_iterations`, and `issue_iterations`. Add pg_trgm extension and comprehensive indexes (GIN on properties, trigram on titles, partial indexes on archived_at/deleted_at).

Write a `seed.ts` that creates a demo workspace, admin user (password hashed with bcryptjs), and sample documents. Write a `migrate.ts` that applies SQL files from `migrations/` in order. The initial canonical migration file must be `api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql`. Verify by running `pnpm db:migrate` and `pnpm db:seed` against a local PostgreSQL instance, then querying each table to confirm it exists with correct columns. Before declaring completion, confirm that `api/db/client.ts`, `api/db/migrate.ts`, `api/db/seed.ts`, `api/db/schema.sql`, and `api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql` all exist.
